ps140: Shining with the radiance of virginity, O honoured Theodosia,* and splendidly adorned with a royal robe dyed in the blood of martyrdom, O all-wise one,* you made your abode in the heavenly bridal-chamber of Christ,* rejoicing with the angelic ranks in perpetual chorus,** O glorious and all-immaculate maiden.

O virgin martyr,* all-praised Theodosia,* desiring Christ with all your soul,* you endured the wounds of martyrdom,* courageously enduring lacerations for Him Whom you loved,* your sides raked with iron claws.* O the steadfast opposition of your struggles,** whereby you truly cast down the prideful one to the ground!

Splendidly adorned with beauty of body and soul,* you brought yourself to Christ, desiring to be crowned by Him with a wreath of glory,* O all-glorious martyr Theodosia,* manifestly receiving upon your brow, as is meet,* the truly priceless diadem of the kingdom,* having been shown to be an incorrupt bride,** O you who are divinely wise.
